commands.author: rendrag-git

target.title: fix(agents): clamp max_completion_tokens to context window for proxy-like openai-completions endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

Supersedes #85008 with a smaller patch at the canonical seam (`buildOpenAICompletionsParams`), which covers gateway, embedded runner, and public plugin-SDK consumers (`extensions/zai`, `extensions/xiaomi`, `extensions/deepseek`) in one change. Fixes #83086.

- **Problem**: OpenClaw can emit an OpenAI Chat Completions output budget that does not fit inside strict OpenAI-compatible server context limits. vLLM, LocalAI, llama.cpp, LM Studio, DashScope, GLM, Kimi, StepFun-style providers, and similar servers reject this before generation with HTTP 400 when `prompt + max_completion_tokens` exceeds their effective context.
- **Why it matters**: Local vLLM and other strict OpenAI-compatible users can see every prompt fail before the model is called. Issue #83086 reports this shape against StepFun step-router-v1 with `context=262144`.
- **What changed**: After `resolveOpenAICompletionsMaxTokens`, proxy-like OpenAI Completions payloads now clamp the resolved output budget to the remaining effective context budget: `max(1, (contextTokens ?? contextWindow) - estimatedInputTokens - 1)`. The input estimate uses the repo's shared CJK-aware `estimateStringChars` helper and applies the safety margin after summing adjusted input chars, so non-Latin prompts are not undercounted and many short messages are not overcounted by per-fragment rounding.
- **Debug proof hook**: When the clamp fires, model transport debug logging now emits `[completions] clamp_max_tokens ... requested=... output=... effectiveContext=... estimatedInput=...`. Enable `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1` to capture the computed outbound cap during live vLLM/strict-provider verification.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: Native OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and OpenRouter behavior remains gated away from this proxy-like clamp. No provider-specific tokenizer or new stream wrapper was added.

## Why a new fix instead of extending #85008

- **Canonical seam**: `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` is the single function that produces the wire payload. Fixing here covers every caller in one place.
- **#85008's interceptor at `pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`** only covers traffic through the embedded runner. Gateway `openclaw infer model run` and external plugin-SDK consumers do not benefit.
- **Estimator ownership**: The builder now uses OpenClaw's existing CJK-aware token-pressure helper instead of a standalone raw `chars / 4` estimate. This keeps the clamp aligned with the repo's documented non-Latin token-pressure behavior.
- **Effective context ownership**: The clamp respects `contextTokens` before `contextWindow`, matching the documented runtime cap used by model config and compaction paths.
- **Pattern precedent**: Same `usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` plumbing as #71472 and the empty-tools fix in #85835. It drops into existing compat infrastructure with no new wrapper.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83086
- Supersedes #85008 (different seam; this PR fixes the canonical builder)
- Related #85835 (same `usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` compat plumbing, same builder, empty-tools fix)
- Related #74738 (preserved `supportsModelTools` gate)
- Related #71472 (same proxy-like detection pattern)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: vLLM rejects OpenClaw OpenAI-compatible chat completions when the outbound output budget does not fit the server's effective context.
- **Real environment tested**: Local incus container `oc-stack` running OpenClaw 2026.5.18 with this patch lineage and the empty-tools fix from #85835 applied to the live `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/openai-transport-stream-*.js` bundle on the pmg gateway. vLLM router served `qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`; `max_model_len` was initially 131072 and later 237568 after a vLLM rollout. Model registered as `vllm/qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`. Route taken: Discord channel -> pm agent -> embedded runner -> openai-completions transport.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Sent ordinary Discord messages in the pm channel with `model.maxTokens` set higher than the effective context. The live route hit the clamp branch, reduced `max_completion_tokens` to the remaining budget, and vLLM accepted the request.
- **Latest-head proof hook for repeat capture**: Current head `1d389c2b5b` adds a gated debug line in the same clamp branch:

  ```text
  [completions] clamp_max_tokens provider=vllm api=openai-completions model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 requested=250000 output=... effectiveContext=... estimatedInput=...
  ```

  Enable `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1` on the live gateway to capture the computed outbound cap alongside the existing `[model-fetch] response ... status=200` line.

- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted runtime logs from the pmg gateway showed the live Discord -> embedded runner -> openai-completions trace returning HTTP 200:

  ```text
  [model-fetch] start provider=vllm api=openai-completions
    model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 method=POST
    url=http://<redacted-vllm-router>/v1/chat/completions
  [model-fetch] response provider=vllm
    model=qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4 status=200 elapsedMs=122
    contentType=text/event-stream; charset=utf-8
  embedded run agent end: runId=... isError=false
  textPreview="Hey! Testing received. How can I help you today?"
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: vLLM returned HTTP 200 with `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and the model produced a normal assistant reply. Before the patch lineage, the same path returned HTTP 400 with the strict-server context-length rejection.
- **What was not tested**: StepFun step-router-v1 specifically (no account); native OpenAI live with an intentionally oversized output cap.
- **Before evidence**: vLLM access log returning `HTTP 400 ... max_completion_tokens=131072 cannot be greater than max_model_len=131072` on the unpatched gateway, captured during diagnostic write-up at `docs/diagnostics/vllm-openclaw-payload-compat-2026-05-23.md`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause**: `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` set `max_completion_tokens` from `resolveOpenAICompletionsMaxTokens(model, options)` with no awareness of prompt size or the effective runtime context cap. When `maxTokens` was at or above the available server context, the wire request asked for more output than strict servers could accept with the prompt.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No test covered proxy-like endpoints where `maxTokens` exceeded the remaining budget after prompt input, CJK prompt text, many short messages, or `contextTokens` lower than `contextWindow`.
- **Contributing context**: Wildcard discovery in `models.providers.<vllm>.models = []` can populate per-model `maxTokens` from provider defaults that match or exceed server `max_model_len`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target test or file**: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- **Scenarios locked in by the tests in this PR**:
  1. Proxy-like endpoint + substantial ASCII prompt + oversized `maxTokens` -> `max_completion_tokens` clamped to remaining budget.
  2. Proxy-like endpoint + CJK prompt -> input estimate uses CJK-aware adjusted chars.
  3. Proxy-like endpoint + many one-character messages -> estimator rounds after summing, not per fragment.
  4. Proxy-like endpoint + lower `contextTokens` -> clamp uses `contextTokens` before `contextWindow`.
  5. Proxy-like endpoint + `maxTokens` already fitting -> unchanged.
  6. Native OpenAI endpoint + oversized `maxTokens` -> unchanged existing behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

For OpenAI-compatible endpoints routed through a proxy-like/non-native route (vLLM, LocalAI, llama.cpp, LM Studio, DashScope, GLM, Kimi, StepFun-style providers, and similar), outbound `max_completion_tokens` is now capped to the remaining effective context budget when the configured output cap would otherwise exceed it. Native OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and OpenRouter behavior remains outside this clamp.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
maxTokens = 250000, effectiveContext = 237568, estimatedInput = 45000
  -> max_completion_tokens: 250000
  -> strict server HTTP 400

After (proxy-like endpoint only):
maxTokens = 250000, effectiveContext = 237568, estimatedInput = 45000
  -> max_completion_tokens: 192567
  -> HTTP 200

contextTokens lower than contextWindow:
contextTokens = 4096, contextWindow = 131072, estimatedInput = 2
  -> max_completion_tokens: 4093
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No` (only payload field value bound on existing calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- **Local unit proof**: Codex worktree `/home/ubuntu/projects/openclaw/.worktrees/clamp-max-tokens`, Node/Vitest via repo wrapper.
- **Live behavior proof**: Ubuntu 22.04 inside incus container `oc-stack`, OpenClaw 2026.5.18 plus this patch lineage, vLLM serving `qwen3-5-122b-a10b-nvfp4`, Discord channel bound to the pm agent.

### Steps

1. Configure a vLLM or similar proxy-like OpenAI-compatible provider with a model entry where `maxTokens` exceeds the effective remaining context.
2. Optionally enable `OPENCLAW_DEBUG_MODEL_TRANSPORT=1` to capture `[completions] clamp_max_tokens ...`.
3. Send a Discord message through the bound agent or run an equivalent gateway request.

### Expected

- Gateway log shows the clamp debug line when enabled.
- Gateway log shows `[model-fetch] response ... status=200`.
- Outbound output cap is reduced to the remaining effective budget.

### Actual

- Matches expected in the live vLLM route.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing log before + passing live behavior after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Focused regression tests
- [x] Local structured review

## Verification Commands

```text
pnpm install
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
git diff --check
```

Latest local results on head `1d389c2b5b`:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       382 passed (382)

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.84)
```

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: End-to-end Discord -> pm agent -> embedded runner -> openai-completions transport against live vLLM with `maxTokens` above the effective context; vLLM accepts the request and the model replies normally.
- **Edge cases checked**: `maxTokens` below budget is unchanged; CJK prompt estimate; many short messages; `contextTokens` lower than `contextWindow`; native OpenAI path unchanged by unit test.
- **What I did not verify**: StepFun step-router-v1 from #83086 (no account); native OpenAI live with intentionally oversized output cap.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- **Backward compatible?** `Yes` for native providers; proxy-like strict providers now receive a smaller, valid output cap where the old payload could fail.
- **Config/env changes?** `No`
- **Migration needed?** `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: The heuristic can still differ from a provider's exact tokenizer.
  - **Mitigation**: The estimate uses the repo's CJK-aware token-pressure helper and a safety margin; wrong-high trims output, wrong-low is what could allow server rejection.
- **Risk**: Very long prompts can still leave only a tiny output budget.
  - **Mitigation**: The clamp floors at 1 token instead of sending an invalid oversized output cap; compaction/pre-prompt pressure remains responsible for broader prompt reduction.
- **Risk**: Users intentionally configured very large `maxTokens` for proxy-like providers.
  - **Mitigation**: The clamp only reduces values that exceed remaining effective context and would be rejected by strict servers.

---

> AI-assisted PR. Investigation, patch authoring, local tests, PR body refresh, and review closeout used Codex. Real behavior proof is from the maintainer's local vLLM/Discord setup; live logs were redacted before inclusion.
target.author: rendrag-git

commands.author: giodl73-repo

target.title: fix(agents): preserve latest thinking replay signatures
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85557.

`stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` now preserves the latest assistant message by default while continuing to strip invalid thinking signatures from older replay turns. That matches the existing `dropThinkingBlocks` invariant and Anthropic's latest-turn replay contract for thinking blocks.

The normal run path now wraps preserve-signature Anthropic-compatible replay in the existing thinking recovery retry, so an opaque provider-side signature rejection retries once without thinking replay instead of wedging the session. Recovery state is request-local, so each provider call can recover once.

Compaction opts out of latest-assistant preservation because it appends a new compaction prompt before provider replay. In that path, stored assistant turns are older history again and invalid signatures are stripped before the compaction request.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` - passed 7 files / 525 tests on rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26324478275, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Anthropic-compatible replay no longer mutates the latest assistant `thinking` or `redacted_thinking` blocks when invalid-signature stripping runs, avoiding provider rejections that require latest assistant thinking content to be replayed unchanged. Older invalid thinking blocks still normalize, and provider-side opaque signature failures retry once without thinking replay.

Real environment tested: WSL2 OpenClaw worktree `/root/src/openclaw-85557` at rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`, plus Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`; `corepack pnpm check:changed` on Testbox `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed 7 files / 525 tests on rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`, covering latest-assistant preservation, older invalid-signature stripping, compaction opt-out for appended replay prompts, Bedrock-style invalid-signature recovery, and request-local recovery. Testbox `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9` changed gate passed core typecheck, core test typecheck, oxlint, import cycles, runtime sidecar, changelog, dependency, and guard lanes.

Observed result after fix: older assistant turns with missing or blank thinking signatures are still stripped or replaced with the omitted-reasoning fallback; the latest assistant turn is preserved unchanged for normal continuation; compaction strips invalid signatures from all stored history before adding its compaction prompt; Anthropic/Bedrock-style thinking signature errors retry once with sanitized recovery history.

What was not tested: live Anthropic or Bedrock API replay with real provider credentials; the proof is source-level plus focused local behavior tests and Linux Testbox changed-gate validation.

## 2026-05-23 update

Rebased onto current `origin/main` at `a1eb765f0a`; refreshed head is `70ec85d145`.

Additional embedded Anthropic-compatible replay proof, run without live provider credentials, using the repo Vitest harness against the same wrapper/sanitizer path used before provider replay:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "preserves invalid thinking signatures on the latest assistant message|can strip invalid thinking signatures from the latest assistant message|retries Bedrock-style invalid thinking signature errors|drops embedded Anthropic user tool_result blocks when signed-thinking replay must stay provider-owned"

✓ stripInvalidThinkingSignatures > preserves invalid thinking signatures on the latest assistant message
✓ stripInvalidThinkingSignatures > can strip invalid thinking signatures from the latest assistant message
✓ wrapAnthropicStreamWithRecovery > retries Bedrock-style invalid thinking signature errors
✓ wrapStreamFnSanitizeMalformedToolCalls > drops embedded Anthropic user tool_result blocks when signed-thinking replay must stay provider-owned

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  5 passed | 318 skipped (323)
```

This targeted proof shows the embedded replay path preserves the latest assistant thinking for normal Anthropic-compatible replay, keeps the explicit compaction/recovery opt-out able to strip it, retries a Bedrock-style invalid-signature provider rejection once, and keeps provider-owned signed-thinking replay from leaking unsafe embedded tool-result blocks.

Fresh validation after rebase:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  7 passed (7)
Tests  525 passed (525)

$ ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ ./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
passed
```

Still not run: live Anthropic or Bedrock API replay with real provider credentials.
